old_claim.note: Early Alchemy & Material Science is an earlier historical predecessor or foundation, not a one-to-one engineering dependency.

new_claim_absent: No alchemy edge remains because the scoped Pseudo-Democritean corpus dates to the first or second century CE, long after the slag-reuse node's 1500 BCE chronology, and the slag source never supported that lineage.

source_shape.source_locator: Chapter title and summary place the Pseudo-Democritean treatise in the first or second century CE and stress uncertainty in its composition.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The corrected alchemical recipe corpus postdates the graph's ancient slag-reuse record by more than a millennium.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: A later text cannot be a historical predecessor of an earlier material practice, and no independent source establishes reverse transmission.

ontology_before: A placeholder-dated broad alchemy node was made an unsupported predecessor of ancient slag reuse.
ontology_after: Slag reuse retains metallurgical inputs without a chronology-breaking dependency on a later alchemical text tradition.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: metallurgical_slag_reuse no longer directly depends on early_alchemy_material_science.
would_reject_if: The alchemical corpus were securely redated before 1500 BCE and shown to transmit slag-reuse practice.
short_reason: The corrected alchemy record is later than slag reuse and cannot precede it.
